info.name: hotel-sim · One guest, one folio — cross-vertical lifecycle to COMPLETED
info.description:
# hotel-sim · One guest, one folio — cross-vertical lifecycle to COMPLETED

One story: **John Patel** checks in, and a **single folio** grows across **all three verticals** (Room + Spa + F&B) and is taken all the way to a **COMPLETED**, fully-settled tab. Read it top to bottom.

## The spine (00 → 08)
- **00** health · **01** create John + open the folio + Room line (£540)
- **02** room card-on-file MANUAL hold (authorised, NOT captured — revenue posts on capture)
- **03** add Spa (£80) — total grows, the room hold doesn't (the deliberate seam)
- **04** add dinner — assert the FROZEN `fnbAttributes` (Slice A5) in F&B availability, then F&B line (£90)
- **05** settle Spa (IMMEDIATE) · **06** settle dinner (IMMEDIATE)
- **07** checkout: capture the room (MANUAL → revenue 0→54000, `customerOwes` 0)
- **08** completeLine ×3 (ungated) → completeFolio (gated) → COMPLETED; final read proves scoped allocation (`netRevenue` == sum of per-line revenue == 71000)

## The appendix — edge probes (green as designed)
- **P1** human-gate (repercussive write without `X-Human-Auth` → 4xx)
- **P2** `lineCoverage` not summing to amount → 400
- **P3** premature `completeFolio` on a fresh ACTIVE-line folio → 409 (C1)
- **P4** idempotent re-complete of the spine folio → 200
- **P5** cancel a fresh uncaptured MANUAL auth → no ledger posting (INV-006)

## How async settlement works here
`capture`, `cancel` and the IMMEDIATE pay chain return **202/200** and `payments-sim` **self-emits** the settlement webhook off-thread; per-line `revenuePosted` lands via the outbox poller. So every settling step calls the action, then **polls** the payment/folio (`setNextRequest`, ≈1s, max 20) until the terminal state — and asserts only then, never on the 202. The only `/v1/test` trigger used is **authorise** (the deferred pay-web stand-in); the real capture/cancel/refund endpoints self-emit, so firing a `/v1/test` trigger for them would 409.

## Run it
1. Bring up the four-service smoke stack (`docker-compose.smoke.yml`); seed products with `seed-products.sql`.
2. `newman run hotel-sim-postman-collection.json` — runs green top to bottom. The spine reaches a COMPLETED folio spanning all three verticals; the five probes pass as designed.

Money is in **pence**. Folders are numbered in story order (00→08, then P1→P5); this replaces the prior folder-by-folder, multi-customer collection that read like a changelog.

item.name: 00 · Lights on
item.description: Health gate — core-api is up before John walks in.

item.name: 01 · John checks in
item.description: Create the guest, open one folio, add the first vertical: a 3-night Deluxe King (£540).

exec: pm.test('booking PENDING, no lines yet', () => pm.expect(f.status).to.eql('PENDING'));

item.name: 3 · Add the ROOM line (Deluxe King, 3 nights, £540)

exec: pm.test('room line revenuePosted 0 (nothing settled yet)', () => pm.expect(room.revenuePosted).to.eql(0));

item.name: 02 · Card on file (MANUAL hold)
item.description: Take a room-scoped MANUAL payment link and let the guest authorise it. The card is HELD, not captured — revenue posts on capture, not auth (INV-006).

item.name: 1 · Create ROOM payment link (MANUAL, scoped to the room line)
2 · [SIM] Guest authorises the link (pay-web stand-in, sync)
3 · Get room payment — AUTHORISED (held, not captured)
4 · Get folio — auth secured, zero revenue

exec: pm.test('amountAuthorised 54000 — live hold (room auth held; only AUTHORISED payment)', () => pm.expect(f.amountAuthorised).to.eql(54000));

item.name: 03 · Add the spa
item.description: A couples massage (£80) joins the SAME folio. The total grows; the room hold does NOT move — the deliberate seam between booked debt and what's been secured on a card.

item.name: 2 · Get folio — total grows to £620, room auth unchanged

exec: pm.test('amountAuthorised STILL 54000 — live hold unchanged (spa line added, no spa payment yet)', () => pm.expect(f.amountAuthorised).to.eql(54000));

item.name: 04 · Add dinner
item.description: Inspect F&B availability — the now-FROZEN fnbAttributes (Slice A5) is populated and the other verticals' attribute blocks stay null on that row — then add a 2-cover dinner (£90) to the same folio. One tab now spans Room + Spa + F&B.

item.name: 1 · Availability (FNB) — fnbAttributes is populated; room/spa attrs null

exec: // Slice A5 — fnbAttributes is FROZEN; assert it is populated
// cross-vertical null integrity: an F&B row carries neither room nor spa attributes
pm.test('roomAttributes null on an F&B row', () => pm.expect(fnb.roomAttributes == null).to.be.true);
pm.test('spaAttributes null on an F&B row', () => pm.expect(fnb.spaAttributes == null).to.be.true);

item.name: 3 · Get folio — one tab, three verticals (£710)

exec: pm.test('three lines on one folio', () => pm.expect(f.lines.length).to.eql(3));

item.name: 05 · Settle spa (IMMEDIATE)
item.description: A spa-scoped IMMEDIATE payment authorises and captures in one chain; the spa line's revenue posts via the outbox. Scoped allocation lands on the spa line alone.

item.name: 1 · Create SPA payment (IMMEDIATE, scoped to the spa line)
2 · [SIM] Guest pays the spa link (IMMEDIATE → auth+capture, sync)
4 · Poll folio until spa line revenue posts (8000)

exec: const settled = pm.response.code === 200 && ((() => { const s = p.lines.find(l => l.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('spaLineId')); return s && s.revenuePosted === 8000; })());
pm.test('room line still 0 (scoped allocation — spa only)', () => { const r = p.lines.find(l => l.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('roomLineId')); pm.expect(r.revenuePosted).to.eql(0); });
pm.test('amountAuthorised 54000 — live hold drops the spent spa auth; captured IMMEDIATE excluded (RX-004; old roll-up would read 62000)', () => pm.expect(p.amountAuthorised).to.eql(54000));

item.name: 06 · Settle dinner (IMMEDIATE)
item.description: Mirror of the spa: an F&B-scoped IMMEDIATE payment captures and the F&B line's £90 posts.

item.name: 1 · Create F&B payment (IMMEDIATE, scoped to the F&B line)
2 · [SIM] Guest pays the F&B link (IMMEDIATE → auth+capture, sync)
4 · Poll folio until F&B line revenue posts (9000)

exec: const settled = pm.response.code === 200 && ((() => { const x = p.lines.find(l => l.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('fnbLineId')); return x && x.revenuePosted === 9000; })());

item.name: 07 · Checkout — capture the room (MANUAL → revenue)
item.description: At checkout the held room auth is captured. The action returns 202 and payments-sim self-emits the CAPTURE webhook off-thread, so we POLL until the payment is CAPTURED and the room line's revenue flips 0 → 54000. After this, customerOwes == 0.

item.name: 1 · Request capture of the room auth (human-auth, 202)

exec: pm.test('202 Accepted (capture requested; PSP self-emits the CAPTURE webhook)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(202));
// Do NOT assert CAPTURED here — settlement lands async on the self-emitted webhook (Trap A).
pm.test('status CAPTURED after self-emitted webhook', () => pm.expect(p.status).to.eql('CAPTURED'));

item.name: 3 · Poll folio until ROOM revenue posts (0 → 54000), owes 0

exec: const settled = pm.response.code === 200 && ((() => { const r = p.lines.find(l => l.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('roomLineId')); return r && r.revenuePosted === 54000 && p.customerOwes === 0; })());
pm.test('room line revenuePosted 54000 (revenue on capture, not auth)', () => pm.expect(room.revenuePosted).to.eql(54000));

item.name: 08 · Close the tab
item.description: Complete each line (ungated), then complete the folio (human-auth gated). C1 (all lines COMPLETED) and C2 (customerOwes == 0) both already hold. The final read proves scoped allocation: each line's revenue sits on its own line; netRevenue == the sum.

item.name: 5 · PROOF — final folio read: scoped allocation, netRevenue == sum

exec: const byId = id => f.lines.find(l => l.id === pm.collectionVariables.get(id));
pm.test('room revenue on the room line (54000)', () => pm.expect(byId('roomLineId').revenuePosted).to.eql(54000));
pm.test('spa revenue on the spa line (8000)', () => pm.expect(byId('spaLineId').revenuePosted).to.eql(8000));
pm.test('F&B revenue on the F&B line (9000)', () => pm.expect(byId('fnbLineId').revenuePosted).to.eql(9000));
const sum = f.lines.reduce((a, l) => a + l.revenuePosted, 0);
pm.test('netRevenue == sum of line revenue (71000)', () => { pm.expect(f.netRevenue).to.eql(71000); pm.expect(sum).to.eql(71000); });
pm.test('amountAuthorised 0 — no live hold; every auth captured (RX-004; old roll-up would read 71000)', () => pm.expect(f.amountAuthorised).to.eql(0));

item.name: P1 · Human-gate
item.description: A repercussive write without X-Human-Auth is refused by the server (INV-007), not trusted to the caller. Re-uses the spine's already-captured room payment — harmless.

item.name: P2 · lineCoverage sum
item.description: A scoped payment whose lineCoverage does not sum to the amount is rejected at write time (400).

exec: pm.test('400 — coverage must sum to amount', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

item.name: P3 · Premature completeFolio (C1)
item.description: completeFolio on a FRESH throwaway folio with an ACTIVE line fails C1 → 409, naming current state. Mints its own state so the spine's completed folio is untouched.

item.name: 2 · Add a ROOM line (non-overlapping window) — leaves it ACTIVE
3 · completeFolio while a line is ACTIVE → 409 (C1)

exec: pm.test('409 — not completable while a line is ACTIVE', () => pm.response.to.have.status(409));
pm.test('conflict reports current state (straggler line / owes)', () => pm.expect(JSON.stringify(e)).to.match(/customerOwes|line|Completable/i));

item.name: P4 · Idempotent re-complete
item.description: completeFolio on the spine's ALREADY-COMPLETED folio is idempotent → 200, not 409.

item.name: P5 · Cancel posts nothing (INV-006)
item.description: Mirror image of the room capture: a FRESH uncaptured MANUAL auth is cancelled. cancel returns 202, payments-sim self-emits the CANCELLATION webhook, we poll to CANCELLED, and prove NO ledger row resulted — cancelling an uncaptured auth is not a financial event.

item.name: 3 · Create a MANUAL payment (scoped to the throwaway line)

exec: pm.test('202 Accepted (cancel requested; PSP self-emits the CANCELLATION webhook)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(202));
pm.test('status CANCELLED after self-emitted webhook', () => pm.expect(p.status).to.eql('CANCELLED'));

item.name: 8 · PROOF — no ledger posting from a cancelled auth (INV-006)

exec: pm.test('netRevenue 0 (no reversal — nothing was posted)', () => pm.expect(f.netRevenue).to.eql(0));

item.name: 1 · getRevenue over the run window — 71000 split by vertical

exec: const v = name => r.byVertical.find(x => x.vertical === name);
// Wide window over a fresh-volume run: the only postings are this collection's three captures.
pm.test('totals: net == gross == 71000, refundedTotal 0 (no refund in the spine)', () => {

item.name: 2 · listUnpaidBookings — settled spine gone; cancelled-auth folio still owes

exec: const find = id => r.bookings.find(b => b.bookingId === pm.collectionVariables.get(id));
// The spine folio is COMPLETED and fully settled (customerOwes 0) -> it has dropped off the worklist.
pm.test('settled spine folio is NOT in the unpaid worklist', () => pm.expect(find('bookingId')).to.eql(undefined));
// P5's throwaway folio held a MANUAL auth that was then CANCELLED. It still owes the full line:
// a held (let alone cancelled) authorisation never reduces owes, and a cancelled auth leaves no
// AUTHORISED payment_line, so lineHeldAuth is 0 too. Proves the captured-only owes rule over HTTP.
pm.test('cancelled-auth folio IS still on the worklist (customerOwes > 0)', () => {
pm.test('its line: revenue 0, held 0 (auth cancelled), owes == full lineAmount', () => {
